name: SmartGrid Optimizer
what_worked: Simple, focused problem: predict energy demand 24 hours ahead
Used free-tier Prophet model - no custom training needed
Clean dashboard with just 3 key metrics (no overwhelming data dump)
Demo with realistic synthetic data showing 15% cost savings
Had fallback cached predictions in case API failed during demo
what_didnt: Initially tried real-time streaming - too complex, scrapped it
Spent 6 hours on fancy animations that judges didn't care about
First database choice (MongoDB) didn't fit time-series data well, migrated to Postgres
key_insight: Judges cared more about 'would this work in production' than flashy tech. Simple + working beats complex + broken.

time_breakdown.hours_0_12: Setup, data pipeline, basic ML integration
time_breakdown.hours_24_36: Polish, handle edge cases, deploy to production
time_breakdown.hours_36_48: Demo rehearsal, backup video, fix last-minute bugs

name: AirQuality Alert

tech_stack.ml: Claude API for natural language alerts

what_worked: Focused on ONE persona: parents with kids who have asthma
SMS alerts when air quality drops (Twilio free tier)
Claude generated human-friendly explanations: 'Air quality is unhealthy - keep windows closed today'
Mobile-first design (judges tested on phones)
Live demo with real public API (PurpleAir sensor network)
what_didnt: Firebase had cold start issues - first query took 8 seconds
Tried to add too many features (pollen, UV index) - cut back to just AQI
Initial SMS wording was too technical - iterated based on mentor feedback
key_insight: Using Claude API for NL generation was huge win - turned technical data into actionable advice. Judges loved the 'friendly assistant' vibe vs raw numbers.

time_breakdown.hours_24_36: Claude integration for NL explanations, mobile responsiveness
time_breakdown.hours_36_48: User testing with mentors, iterating on copy

name: FoodWaste Tracker

tech_stack.ml: OpenAI GPT-4 Vision for receipt scanning

what_worked: GPT-4 Vision scanned grocery receipts and extracted items automatically (no manual entry)
Sent expiration reminders via email (simple cron job)
Carbon footprint estimates per item (referenced USDA database)
Worked entirely offline after initial setup (SQLite local-first)
Pitch emphasized '$500/year savings per household' - judges loved concrete ROI
what_didnt: GPT-4 Vision API was expensive - burned through $15 credit in testing
Receipt scanning accuracy was 80% - needed manual corrections
Tried to add meal planning feature - ran out of time, cut it
key_insight: The 'magic moment' (scan receipt, instant inventory) carried the demo. Everything else was secondary. Nail ONE wow factor.

time_breakdown.hours_0_12: Receipt parsing with GPT-4 Vision, data model design
time_breakdown.hours_36_48: Demo polish, backup images for receipt scanning

name: WaterSense Monitor
what_worked: Streamlit = 0 frontend code needed, pure Python full-stack
Deployed in 10 minutes (literally just `streamlit run app.py`)
Anomaly detection found 'leaky irrigation zones' saving 20% water
Interactive sliders let judges explore different scenarios
All synthetic data (convincingly realistic using Faker library)
what_didnt: Streamlit UI looks generic - no custom branding
In-memory data = lost state on refresh (needed persistence)
key_insight: Speed matters. Streamlit let us iterate 3x faster than building separate frontend/backend. Trade polish for velocity in hackathons.

name: BusyBee (Coworking Occupancy Predictor)
what_worked: Predicted coworking space occupancy to help remote workers plan their day
TensorFlow.js ran predictions client-side (no backend ML infrastructure)
Google Calendar integration showed 'your usual crowd' is coming today
Live desk availability heatmap (updated every 5 min)
Judges loved the 'social' angle - not just optimization, but community
what_didnt: Heroku free tier spun down after 30 min idle - caused lag during judging
MongoDB schema was overcomplicated - should've used Postgres
Spent 8 hours on auth (OAuth) that wasn't critical for demo
key_insight: Winning pitch framed it as 'Waze for coworking' - familiar analogy made judges immediately get it. Analogies > technical explanations.

time_breakdown.hours_36_48: Demo flow rehearsal, backup static predictions
